{"result":"     1	-- Semantic Type Predicate Macros
     2	-- Convenience functions for filtering by specific semantic types
     3	-- These wrap is_semantic_type() for cleaner queries
     4	
     5	-- =============================================================================
     6	-- Definition Predicates
     7	-- =============================================================================
     8	
     9	-- Check if semantic_type is a function definition
    10	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_function_definition(st) AS
    11	    is_semantic_type(st, 'DEFINITION_FUNCTION');
    12	
    13	-- Check if semantic_type is a class definition
    14	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_class_definition(st) AS
    15	    is_semantic_type(st, 'DEFINITION_CLASS');
    16	
    17	-- Check if semantic_type is a variable definition
    18	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_variable_definition(st) AS
    19	    is_semantic_type(st, 'DEFINITION_VARIABLE');
    20	
    21	-- Check if semantic_type is a module/namespace definition
    22	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_module_definition(st) AS
    23	    is_semantic_type(st, 'DEFINITION_MODULE');
    24	
    25	-- Check if semantic_type is a type definition (typedef, type alias)
    26	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_type_definition(st) AS
    27	    is_semantic_type(st, 'DEFINITION_TYPE');
    28	
    29	-- =============================================================================
    30	-- Computation Predicates
    31	-- =============================================================================
    32	
    33	-- Check if semantic_type is a function/method call
    34	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_function_call(st) AS
    35	    is_semantic_type(st, 'COMPUTATION_CALL');
    36	
    37	-- Check if semantic_type is a member/property access
    38	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_member_access(st) AS
    39	    is_semantic_type(st, 'COMPUTATION_ACCESS');
    40	
    41	-- =============================================================================
    42	-- Literal Predicates
    43	-- =============================================================================
    44	
    45	-- Check if semantic_type is a string literal
    46	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_string_literal(st) AS
    47	    is_semantic_type(st, 'LITERAL_STRING');
    48	
    49	-- Check if semantic_type is a number literal
    50	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_number_literal(st) AS
    51	    is_semantic_type(st, 'LITERAL_NUMBER');
    52	
    53	-- Check if semantic_type is a boolean literal
    54	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_boolean_literal(st) AS
    55	    is_semantic_type(st, 'LITERAL_BOOLEAN');
    56	
    57	-- Check if semantic_type is any literal type
    58	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_literal(st) AS
    59	    is_semantic_type(st, 'LITERAL');
    60	
    61	-- =============================================================================
    62	-- Flow Control Predicates
    63	-- =============================================================================
    64	
    65	-- Check if semantic_type is a conditional (if/switch/match)
    66	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_conditional(st) AS
    67	    is_semantic_type(st, 'FLOW_CONDITIONAL');
    68	
    69	-- Check if semantic_type is a loop (for/while/do)
    70	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_loop(st) AS
    71	    is_semantic_type(st, 'FLOW_LOOP');
    72	
    73	-- Check if semantic_type is a jump (return/break/continue/throw)
    74	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_jump(st) AS
    75	    is_semantic_type(st, 'FLOW_JUMP');
    76	
    77	-- =============================================================================
    78	-- Organization Predicates
    79	-- =============================================================================
    80	
    81	-- Check if semantic_type is a block/scope
    82	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_block(st) AS
    83	    is_semantic_type(st, 'ORGANIZATION_BLOCK');
    84	
    85	-- Check if semantic_type is a list/array/container
    86	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO is_list(st) AS
    87	    is_semantic_type(st, 'ORGANIZATION_LIST');
    88	
    89	-- =============================================================================
    90	-- Operator Predicates

{"result":"     1	-- File Utility Macros
     2	-- Scalar functions for extracting source code from files
     3	--
     4	-- For full-featured line reading (globs, line specs, context windows, lateral joins),
     5	-- use the duckdb_read_lines extension: https://github.com/teaguesterling/duckdb_read_lines
     6	
     7	-- =============================================================================
     8	-- Source Extraction - Scalar Macros (return single value)
     9	-- =============================================================================
    10	
    11	-- Extract source code for an AST node given file_path, start_line, end_line
    12	-- Returns lines as a single newline-joined string
    13	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_get_source(file_path, start_line, end_line) AS (
    14	    WITH file_content AS (
    15	        SELECT string_split(content, E'\n') as lines
    16	        FROM read_text(file_path)
    17	    ),
    18	    line_nums AS (
    19	        SELECT lines, generate_series(1, len(lines)) as line_numbers
    20	        FROM file_content
    21	    ),
    22	    all_lines AS (
    23	        SELECT
    24	            UNNEST(line_numbers) AS line_number,
    25	            lines[UNNEST(line_numbers)] AS line
    26	        FROM line_nums
    27	    )
    28	    SELECT string_agg(line, E'\n' ORDER BY line_number)
    29	    FROM all_lines
    30	    WHERE line_number >= start_line AND line_number <= end_line
    31	);
    32	
    33	-- Get source with line numbers prefixed (useful for display)
    34	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_get_source_numbered(file_path, start_line, end_line) AS (
    35	    WITH file_content AS (
    36	        SELECT string_split(content, E'\n') as lines
    37	        FROM read_text(file_path)
    38	    ),
    39	    line_nums AS (
    40	        SELECT lines, generate_series(1, len(lines)) as line_numbers
    41	        FROM file_content
    42	    ),
    43	    all_lines AS (
    44	        SELECT
    45	            UNNEST(line_numbers) AS line_number,
    46	            lines[UNNEST(line_numbers)] AS line
    47	        FROM line_nums
    48	    )
    49	    SELECT string_agg(
    50	        printf('%4d: %s', line_number, line),
    51	        E'\n' ORDER BY line_number
    52	    )
    53	    FROM all_lines
    54	    WHERE line_number >= start_line AND line_number <= end_line
    55	);
    56	
    57	-- =============================================================================
    58	-- Source Lookup - Table Macro (find definition by name, return numbered source)
    59	-- =============================================================================
    60	
    61	-- Look up a definition by name and return its numbered source code
    62	-- Usage:
    63	--   SELECT * FROM ast_source_of('src/**/*.py', 'process_payment');
    64	--   SELECT * FROM ast_source_of('src/**/*.py', 'MyClass', kind := 'class');
    65	--   SELECT * FROM ast_source_of('src/**/*.py', 'handler', language := 'python', kind := 'function');
    66	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_source_of(
    67	    file_patterns,
    68	    target_name,
    69	    language := NULL,
    70	    kind := NULL
    71	) AS TABLE
    72	    WITH
    73	        ast AS (
    74	            SELECT *
    75	            FROM read_ast(file_patterns, language)
    76	        ),
    77	        matches AS (
    78	            SELECT
    79	                file_path,
    80	                name,
    81	                CASE
    82	                    WHEN is_function_definition(semantic_type) THEN 'function'
    83	                    WHEN is_class_definition(semantic_type) THEN 'class'
    84	                    WHEN is_variable_definition(semantic_type) THEN 'variable'
    85	                    WHEN is_module_definition(semantic_type) THEN 'module'
    86	                    WHEN is_type_definition(semantic_type) THEN 'type'
    87	                    ELSE 'other'
    88	                END AS definition_kind,
    89	                type,
    90	                start_line,
    91	                end_line
    92	            FROM ast
    93	            WHERE is_definition(semantic_type)
    94	              AND is_construct(flags)
    95	              AND name = target_name
    96	              AND (kind IS NULL
    97	                   OR (kind = 'function' AND is_function_definition(semantic_type))
    98	                   OR (kind = 'class' AND is_class_definition(semantic_type))
    99	                   OR (kind = 'variable' AND is_variable_definition(semantic_type))
   100	                   OR (kind = 'module' AND is_module_definition(semantic_type))
   101	                   OR (kind = 'type' AND is_type_definition(semantic_type))
   102	              )
   103	        ),
   104	        -- Read file contents for all matched files
   105	        file_contents AS (
   106	            SELECT DISTINCT ON (m.file_path)
   107	                m.file_path,
   108	                string_split(fc.content, E'\n') AS lines
   109	            FROM matches m
   110	            JOIN read_text(file_patterns) fc ON fc.filename = m.file_path
   111	        ),
   112	        -- Build numbered source for each match
   113	        source_output AS (
   114	            SELECT
   115	                m.file_path,
   116	                m.name,
   117	                m.definition_kind,
   118	                m.start_line,
   119	                m.end_line,
   120	                (SELECT string_agg(
   121	                    printf('%4d: %s', line_num, fc.lines[line_num]),
   122	                    E'\n' ORDER BY line_num
   123	                )
   124	                FROM generate_series(m.start_line, m.end_line) AS t(line_num)
   125	                ) AS source
   126	            FROM matches m
   127	            JOIN file_contents fc ON fc.file_path = m.file_path
   128	        )
   129	    SELECT
   130	        file_path,
   131	        name,
   132	        definition_kind,
   133	        start_line,
   134	        end_line,
   135	        source
   136	    FROM source_output
   137	    ORDER BY file_path, start_line;
   138	
   139	-- =============================================================================
   140	-- Source Extraction - Single Line
   141	-- =============================================================================
   142	
   143	-- Get a single line from a file
   144	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_get_source_line(file_path, line_num) AS (
   145	    WITH file_content AS (
   146	        SELECT string_split(content, E'\n') as lines
   147	        FROM read_text(file_path)
   148	    )
   149	    SELECT lines[line_num]
   150	    FROM file_content
   151	);
"}

    51	    )

    56	            WHEN is_class_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'class'
    57	            WHEN is_variable_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'variable'
    58	            WHEN is_module_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'module'
    59	            WHEN is_type_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'type'
    60	            ELSE 'other'
    61	        END AS definition_type,
    62	        a.language,
    63	        a.file_path,
    64	        a.start_line,
    65	        a.end_line,
    66	        a.node_id,
    67	        a.type,
    68	        a.semantic_type
    69	    FROM ast a
    70	    WHERE is_definition(a.semantic_type)
    71	      AND is_construct(a.flags)
    72	      AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
    73	    ORDER BY a.file_path, a.start_line;
    74	
    75	-- Get all descendants of a node (entire subtree)
    76	-- Uses descendant_count for O(1) range-based lookup (nodes are in DFS pre-order)
    77	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_descendants(my_ast_table, ancestor_node_id)
    78	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_descendants(ast_table, ancestor_node_id) AS TABLE
    79	    WITH ancestor AS (
    80	        SELECT node_id, descendant_count
    81	        FROM query_table(ast_table)
    82	        WHERE node_id = ancestor_node_id
    83	    )
    84	    SELECT a.*
    85	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, ancestor anc
    86	    WHERE a.node_id > anc.node_id
    87	      AND a.node_id <= anc.node_id + anc.descendant_count;
    88	
    89	-- Get ancestors of a node (path from node to root)
    90	-- Uses recursive CTE following parent_id upward
    91	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_ancestors(my_ast_table, child_node_id)
    92	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_ancestors(ast_table, child_node_id) AS TABLE
    93	    WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS (
    94	        SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = child_node_id
    95	        UNION ALL
    96	        SELECT a.*
    97	        FROM query_table(ast_table) a
    98	        JOIN ancestors anc ON a.node_id = anc.parent_id
    99	        WHERE anc.parent_id IS NOT NULL
   100	    )
"}
